description: Replays a non-timeout-shaped failed-builder run through decomposer via triggerPayload and asserts assess-failure short-circuits to shouldDecompose: false (decompose agent step skipped, no commit, no restart). Proves the triggerPayload subprocess plumbing forwards verbatim and the decomposer decision gate stays calibrated, without paying for any agent call.

provenance.justification: Locks two harness-plumbing invariants no real-failure fixture captures today and that no other smoke fixture covers either: (1) triggerPayload round-trips verbatim through `kota workflow exec --payload <json>` so the runDir/runId of the upstream failed-builder run reach decomposer's assess-failure step intact (the dispatcher smoke fixture only exercises the payload-free `manual` trigger), and (2) decomposer's assess-failure decision gate returns shouldDecompose: false on a non-timeout-shaped builder failure, skipping the decompose agent step (asserted directly against the deterministic decomposer run metadata so a regression that lets the agent step run is caught loudly without a real LLM call). The fixture uses the `_runId` field of the queue-replay protocol to pin the decomposer run dir name so file-contains can inspect the metadata.json without globbing — `_runId` is part of the payload the runtime-dispatch path naturally adds to every queued workflow.completed trigger, so this is the same shape decomposer sees in production. The agent-call path (shouldDecompose: true → real decompose run) remains uncovered pending the agent-step bootstrap follow-up tracked in fixtures/uncovered/notes.md.
